description: This cross-sectional micrograph displays small oxide nodules formed on the Fe-10Mn-5Al alloy following isothermal oxidation at 800°C for 24 hours in pure oxygen (200 torr), as stated in the caption. The image, marked with a visible 7 μm scale bar, shows several distinct nodules protruding from the light alloy substrate into the dark gas phase. The caption reports that the nodule structure consists of a grey manganese oxide layer at the gas-oxide interface above a white Fe2O3 layer, with aluminum-rich particles present within this white layer and a continuous aluminum-rich healing layer at the nodule-alloy interface. This morphology represents the small nodule structure that appears when manganese content exceeds approximately 7.5 wt%, marking a transition from the large iron-rich nodules observed on lower-manganese alloys. The micrograph provides visual evidence of the complex layered oxide architecture and supports the finding that manganese enriches at the outer gas interface rather than forming bulky iron oxides in this composition range.
